blockingIssues.description: VAL-SEC-002 is not fully satisfied: Slack authorization is only enforced when `authorizedSlackUserIds.length > 0`. When the list is empty (the default from config parsing), messages from any Slack user are still accepted for injection, so the original trust-boundary issue remains for default/misconfigured setups.
evidence: src/notifications/config.ts:890-893 parses `authorizedSlackUserIds`; parser returns `[]` when unset/invalid.
validation-contract.md:10-13 requires author ID verification against an authorized list, matching Discord's authorization pattern.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Most newly added Slack authorization tests are implementation-string checks or array behavior checks rather than end-to-end listener behavior tests. This weakens regression confidence and allowed the conditional-bypass behavior to pass tests.
evidence: Tests at ~551+ assert `includes` behavior and `source` string contains checks, not actual message-processing outcomes through the listener path.

codeReview.summary: VAL-SEC-001 and VAL-SEC-003 are correctly implemented with clear code changes and regression tests. VAL-SEC-002 remains incomplete because authorization is optional at runtime (empty list allows all users), which does not fully close the reported security gap.

issues.description: Conditional Slack authorization (`length > 0`) leaves unrestricted injection path when no authorized list is configured.
Regression tests are largely static/source assertions and do not verify runtime reject/accept behavior in the Slack listener pipeline.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Expected shared service registry file is missing.
Expected shared architecture/library directory is missing, which limits standardized architecture-reference checks during scrutiny.

Feature references `skillName: bugfix-worker`, but no corresponding skill definition file is discoverable in the repository or user skill directories, reducing auditable traceability of procedure compliance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json lists `skillName: bugfix-worker`; Glob for `**/bugfix-worker/SKILL.md` under /home/paul and /home/paul/.factory returned no matches.

commands: Grep checks for VAL-SEC implementation lines in target files

summary: Review failed on VAL-SEC-002 due to conditional Slack auth enforcement that still permits unrestricted injection when no authorized user list is configured. VAL-SEC-001 and VAL-SEC-003 appear correctly implemented.
